sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 47-year-old male, presents with a 2-hour history of low back pain and right leg tingling, which began after raking leaves in his yard.
He describes the pain as tightening up in his back and radiating to his right foot, with some improvement with a hot shower and sitting.
He reports no prior similar episodes, but a similar episode 3 years ago resolved after a day.
He denies taking any pain medication, but has tried ice and 2 Advil, which did not help.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: Not mentioned.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's dorsiflexion is normal, and pulses are equal in all extremities.
The patient reports some tingling in his right leg.
The patient has some pain on flexion and palpation around the L5 area.
Test Results: Normal X-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a probable sprain of the low back.
Problem list: Acute low back strain; suspected radiculopathy; unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg PO q12h for pain and inflammation.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve mobility and strength.
The patient will follow up with me in 2 weeks to re-evaluate the condition and consider an MRI if the symptoms do not improve.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 2 weeks to re-evaluate the condition and consider further testing, such as an MRI, if necessary.
